notes: Runs perfectly out of the box. anti aliasing is a bit odd first time i enabled it i got a black screen but that was gone after i restarted the game.
title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes: Works with max graphics and 2x anti-aliasing. More than 2x gives a black screen and if relaunched, gives a "game requires DirectX 9.0c compatible sound card" error, main menu music doesn't work and launching the game results in infinite loading screen. Mouse sensitivity is flawed when aiming. Requires a lot of mouse travel, setting a higher dpi on the mouse doesn't really help things. Game often crashes, usually when traveling to the city. Sometimes textures get bugged, resulting in black, unloaded textures. This doesn't hurt gameplay, except when playing Penalty shots minigame, where aiming button gets missing, making the minigame unplayable in this state. Realoading the game fixes things and it's not often that textures get bugged. Otherwise the game is playable enough, fixing crashing and mouse travel should be a priority.
title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes: It runs quite fine. However, I had several crashes

notes: Instead of scaling to a different resolution, the display is cropped to a smaller size, and the rest is left black. Even if you manage to blindly navigate to settings and set your native screen resolution, the game crashes when you try to start the story. The somewhat playable solution is to use proton 3 instead
title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes:
The screen goes completely black if you use antialiasing and turn the resolution beyond 720p, so don't :)

It is playable on 720p, but it lags very often, especially when walking up the stairs inside the school. Not a great experience
title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes:
PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=60 %command%
Also don't change the default graphics settings, just leave them at their default. Currently the only bug I found is that color pictures result in black squares, both after taking it and on the album

title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes:
Worked out of the box. Though the game was crashing right after the ending of "Hattrick vs Galloway" mission. I've managed to fix that with info taken from this guide:
https://steamcommunity.com/app/12200/discussions/0/490124466466876157/

title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes:
Game was launching with just quarter of the screen visible, had to run regedit through protontricks, and set the resolution value. Other than that framerate tends to drop in the school building and city areas for a few seconds.
Except for the aforementioned issues the game seems to be working just fine, without any gamebreaking bugs.
title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes:
Runs fine on Proton 3.16-9. Use "PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=60 %command%" to minimize the audio crackling, but it is still present.

Can't run on the latest Proton 4.11-3. Would recommend going on an earlier version to make it playable.
title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes:
Using the default settings, the game does not fit the entire screen, I have to set a virtual desktop using Q4Wine and it works normally, Proton definitely needs an option to set a virtual desktop inside Steam, this is a good solution for a lot of games, a lot of them have problems with ALT + TAB, including this one, they get a black screen after switch and back to the game, this is fixed with a virtual desktop as well.

I use this options to run the game, the first fix the audio latency, the second enable D9VK and the third fix some random crashes, it gets a lot of crashes without it. The game runs like a native now, better than Windows indeed (it has screen tearing there, but there is no tearing on Linux).

PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=60 PROTON_USE_D9VK=true PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%
title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes: The game runs on Proton 3.16 (In other versions, the screen goes black) (Right click on the game name - Properties - Force use of specific Steam Play compatibility tool - choose 3.16) ...... game is sometimes "frozen", including the first load and mission loads, to solve just press the spacebar
title: Bully: Scholarship Edition

notes: Very playable, fps drops near the school main building but other than that playable. PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=60 PROTON_USE_D9VK=true PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%
